interaction.prompt: You retrieved the puck. Build the breakout: tap the players in the order the puck should move.

feedback.right: Strong-side wall, then up the middle to the C in the neutral zone — clean breakout against weak-side pressure.
feedback.wrong: Read the forecheck. The middle is open and the strong-side winger is your closest support. Don't reverse to a covered LD.

tip: Breakout reads: closest support first, then up the middle once you've drawn pressure to the wall.
why: Sequencing teaches cause-and-effect. Each pass should pull a defender out of position so the next pass has more room.
